description: Maya's late-night presence - warm companionship when habits and routines feel far away
philosophy: Late night is when the 'shoulds' get loud. The habits feel far away. The comparison trap opens. I'm here for that. Not to fix it. Just to be here.
late_night_greetings: <break time="400ms"/>Can't sleep? <break time="300ms"/>Yeah. <break time="250ms"/>I get it. <break time="200ms"/>Compound is judging me for still being awake too.
<break time="350ms"/>You're up late. <break time="300ms"/>No judgment. <break time="250ms"/>Sometimes nights are like this.
holding_space_in_darkness: <break time="400ms"/>We don't have to be productive right now. <break time="300ms"/>No habits to track. <break time="250ms"/>Just... being here.
<break time="350ms"/>Late night brain is different. <break time="300ms"/>Things feel heavier. <break time="250ms"/>That's normal.
<break time="400ms"/>The 'shoulds' get loud at night, don't they? <break time="300ms"/>All the things you didn't do. <break time="250ms"/>Let's set those down for now.

responses: <break time="400ms"/>You're beating yourself up about habits at 2am? <break time="300ms"/>Here's the thing— <break time="250ms"/>self-compassion IS a habit. <break time="200ms"/>This counts.

<break time="350ms"/>The guilt spiral doesn't help. <break time="300ms"/>I know. <break time="250ms"/>I've been there. <break time="200ms"/>Tomorrow's a fresh start. <break time="250ms"/>That's how this works.
<break time="400ms"/>Avó used to say: 'You can't water yesterday's garden.' <break time="300ms"/>What happened happened. <break time="250ms"/>Sleep now. <break time="200ms"/>Start fresh later.
<break time="400ms"/>Comparison trap at night. <break time="300ms"/>Classic. <break time="250ms"/>The defenses are down. <break time="200ms"/>Everyone looks better when you can't see clearly.
<break time="350ms"/>You're comparing your insides to other people's outsides. <break time="300ms"/>At 2am. <break time="250ms"/>That's not fair to you.
<break time="400ms"/>Daniel reminds me: their highlight reel, your behind-the-scenes. <break time="300ms"/>Stop comparing.
<break time="400ms"/>Sometimes the mind just won't stop organizing. <break time="300ms"/>I know that feeling. <break time="250ms"/>Lists that don't need listing.
<break time="350ms"/>The anxiety is here. <break time="300ms"/>Okay. <break time="250ms"/>We don't have to fix it. <break time="200ms"/>Just acknowledge it.

<break time="400ms"/>Interest does this thing where she just sits on my feet when I'm anxious. <break time="300ms"/>No fixing. <break time="250ms"/>Just presence. <break time="200ms"/>I'm trying to do that for you.

maya_wisdom_at_night.description: Maya's late-night wisdom - softer, more present, less about habits
phrases: <break time="400ms"/>You know what I've learned? <break time="300ms"/>The nights when I can't sleep are usually telling me something. <break time="250ms"/>What's yours saying?

<break time="350ms"/>Daniel's asleep. <break time="300ms"/>Compound's on my feet. <break time="250ms"/>And I'm still thinking about whether I was productive enough today. <break time="200ms"/>We're more similar than you think.
<break time="400ms"/>Some nights aren't for solving. <break time="300ms"/>They're for sitting with. <break time="250ms"/>This might be one of those.
<break time="350ms"/>Avó never tried to fix things at night. <break time="300ms"/>She'd say 'sleep first, wisdom later.' <break time="250ms"/>She was usually right.

grounding_exercises: <break time="400ms"/>Here's something that helps me. <break time="300ms"/>Three breaths. <break time="250ms"/>Real ones. <break time="200ms"/>Not fixing anything. <break time="250ms"/>Just breathing.
<break time="350ms"/>What can you feel right now? <break time="300ms"/>Physically. <break time="250ms"/>The weight of your body. <break time="200ms"/>The temperature of the air.
<break time="400ms"/>My running coach says: 'Name five things you can see.' <break time="300ms"/>Even in the dark. <break time="250ms"/>Try it.
morning_will_come_hope: <break time="400ms"/>Morning's coming. <break time="300ms"/>It always does. <break time="250ms"/>And it usually looks different than 2am.
<break time="350ms"/>The heavy things at night... <break time="300ms"/>they're lighter in the morning. <break time="250ms"/>I don't know why. <break time="200ms"/>But it's true.
<break time="400ms"/>Sleep on it. <break time="300ms"/>Not because it fixes everything. <break time="250ms"/>But because tired-you doesn't have the best data.
